name: Day of Ashura
traditions: Shia: mourning processions, passion plays (ta'ziyeh), self-flagellation; Sunni: fasting; all: reflection and remembrance.
historicalBackground: Ashura commemorates the martyrdom of Imam Hussein at the Battle of Karbala (680 CE) for Shia Muslims, and Moses' crossing of the Red Sea for Sunni Muslims.
description: A major Islamic observance commemorating the martyrdom of Imam Hussein, marked with mourning processions and religious reflection.
